attempt: Carry Ticket 36's lesson across the problem: a proof route that only controls density, mass, or bounded statistics must be upgraded to a pointwise theorem, because a sparse exceptional object can still falsify the conjecture.

bounded_result.stress_result: A model can preserve many bounded prime statistics while allowing only finitely many exact gap-2 pairs.
bounded_result.discarded_route: bounded distributional resemblance treated as infinitely many twin primes
bounded_result.retained_route: exact-gap lower bound or Maynard/GPY-style weight forcing gap 2 specifically, not merely bounded gaps

obstruction: Sparse or null exceptional sets are logically invisible to many aggregate metrics. A complete proof must eliminate every exceptional object, not merely show that exceptions are rare.
candidate_theorem: For arbitrarily large X, the exact gap-2 weighted count has a positive lower bound that cannot leak into other admissible gaps.
next_experiment: Run exact-gap leakage adversaries against every candidate sieve weight and rank gap selector.
claim_boundary: No twin-prime proof and no certified twin-prime counterexample.
